title: Apple Cinnamon Porridge
description: A Chef Aly Thermomix reinterpretation of Annabel Langbein-style apple cinnamon porridge: creamy oats, grated apple and warm spice with exact TM6 and TM7 settings.
intro: Annabel Langbein does simple comfort beautifully, and this porridge is exactly that kind of breakfast. The Thermomix grates the apple, cooks the oats gently and keeps the texture creamy without you standing at the stove stirring.
ingredients: 1 tbsp maple syrup or brown sugar, plus extra to serve
30 g sultanas or chopped dates, optional
40 g toasted walnuts or almonds, to serve
Extra milk or yoghurt, to serve
steps: Place the apple quarters in the mixing bowl and grate 3 sec / 0°C / speed 5. Scrape down so the apple sits evenly around the blades.
Add oats, milk, water, cinnamon, maple syrup, salt and sultanas if using. Cook 12 min / 90°C / Reverse / speed 1, MC on, until the oats are creamy and the apple has softened.
Rest in the bowl 2 min / 0°C / Reverse / speed 0 so the oats thicken gently without catching.
Stir 10 sec / 0°C / Reverse / speed 2 to loosen and distribute the apple through the porridge.
Serve warm 1 min / 0°C / no speed with extra milk or yoghurt, toasted nuts and a little extra maple syrup if you like.
tips: Use rolled oats, not instant oats, for the best creamy texture without turning gluey.
For a softer apple flavour, cook the grated apple with the oats; for freshness, grate half an apple over the top at the end.
If the porridge thickens as it sits, stir through 30–60 g extra milk before serving.

faq.q: Can I make apple porridge in a Thermomix?
faq.a: Yes. The Thermomix grates the apple first, then cooks the oats gently at 90°C on Reverse so the porridge stays creamy rather than blended.

faq.q: Can I make this porridge dairy-free?
faq.a: Yes. Use oat milk, almond milk or soy milk in place of dairy milk, keeping the same total liquid quantity.

faq.q: Is this the original Annabel Langbein recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Annabel Langbein and apple porridge; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.
